title: Optimal Cash Transfers and Microinsurance to Reduce Social Protection Costs
abstract: arXiv:2511.07431v3 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Design and implementation of appropriate social protection strategies is one of the main targets of the United Nation's Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 1: No Poverty. Cash transfer (CT) programmes are considered one of the main social protection strategies and an instrument for achieving SDG 1. Targeting consists of establishing eligibility criteria for beneficiaries of CT programmes. In low-income countries, where resources are limited, proper targeting of CTs is essential for an efficient use of resources. Given the growing importance of microinsurance as a complementary tool to social protection strategies, this study examines its role as a supplement to CT programmes. In this article, we adopt the piecewise-deterministic Markov process introduced in Kovacevic and Pflug (2011) to model the capital of a household, which when exposed to proportional capital losses (in contrast to the classical Cram\'er-Lundberg model) can push th

Title: Optimal Cash Transfers and Microinsurance to Reduce Social Protection Costs

translated_title: 最优现金转移与小额保险以降低社会保护成本
translated_abstract: arXiv:2511.07431v3 公告类型：替换 摘要：设计和实施适当的社会保护策略是联合国可持续发展目标1（无贫困）的主要目标之一。现金转移项目被视为主要的社会保护策略之一，也是实现可持续发展目标1的工具。目标定位涉及为现金转移项目的受益者建立资格标准。在资源有限的低收入国家，现金转移的精准定位对于高效利用资源至关重要。鉴于小额保险作为社会保护策略补充工具的重要性日益增加，本研究探讨了其作为现金转移项目补充的作用。在本文中，我们采用了Kovacevic和Pflug（2011）引入的分段确定性马尔可夫过程来建模家庭资本，当家庭资本面临比例资本损失（与经典的Cramér-Lundberg模型不同）时，这可能导致家庭陷入贫困。
tldr: 本研究通过分段确定性马尔可夫过程模型，探讨了小额保险作为现金转移项目补充工具在降低社会保护成本中的最优作用，为低收入国家资源高效利用提供了新策略。
en_tdlr: This study uses a piecewise-deterministic Markov process model to explore the optimal role of microinsurance as a supplement to cash transfer programs in reducing social protection costs, offering a new strategy for efficient resource use in low-income countries.
